channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Claude Code, the Pricing Gap, and the Rise of Build-Your-Own AI
item.description: On April 21, 2026, Anthropic quietly removed Claude Code from its $20 Pro plan. No email, no announcement, no changelog. People noticed because the pricing page changed overnight. A few hours...

item.title: How to Extract Colors from Images Using JavaScript 🎨
item.description: I wrote a step-by-step guide on building a color palette extractor using JavaScript. Covers: Canvas API Pixel data extraction Dominant color logic Read here:...

item.title: 🚀 REST vs gRPC Performance in Go: A Practical Benchmark-Driven Guide
item.description: When building high-performance microservices in Go, one question inevitably comes up: Should you use REST or gRPC? This isn’t just an architectural debate it directly impacts latency, throughput,...

item.title: Probability: How AI Deals With Uncertainty
item.description: Your spam filter does not know if an email is spam. It cannot know for certain. Nobody can. The word "free" appears in legitimate emails. The word "urgent" appears in real emergencies. No single...

item.title: GPU Prices Up 48% in Two Months. I Run LLMs in My Garage.
item.description: Tom Tunguz published a piece this week on GPU compute scarcity. The numbers are wild. The cloud GPU crisis Nvidia Blackwell rental: $4.08/hr. Two months ago it was $2.75. That's a 48% increase....

item.title: Reading PostgreSQL EXPLAIN and EXPLAIN ANALYZE Output
item.description: Every PostgreSQL performance conversation eventually lands on a question that sounds trivial: what does this EXPLAIN mean? The output is almost readable. There are node names in English, numbers...

item.title: Claude Code caching is eating your budget. Here's what's happening.
item.description: Claude Code caching is eating your budget. Here's what's happening. Claude Code's prompt caching has two TTLs (5 minutes and 1 hour), two price tiers (25% and 100% premium on cache writes), and...

item.description: We were 8 weeks into a sales cycle with a Fortune 500 company. $240K ACV deal. Our biggest ever. The champion loved our product. The technical evaluation went great. The pilot was successful. Then...

item.title: The Complete Guide to Public Status Pages for Your Business in 2026
item.description: When your product breaks, your customers are already refreshing. They are checking Twitter, pinging your support inbox, and wondering whether the problem is on their end or yours. The single...

item.title: pyproject.toml: Modern Python Dependency Management
item.description: pyproject.toml is the contract behind modern Python dependency management. Without it, installers have to execute setup.py or guess build requirements, which leads to fragile builds and...

item.title: The Trump phone still isn’t real
item.description: Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. We've reached out, as usual, to ask about the Trump phone's whereabouts. We're back to being ignored, and the phone seems...

item.title: I don’t think Gwyneth Paltrow knows what a peptide is

item.title: Nothing introduces an AI-powered dictation tool
item.description: Nothing's new on-device dictation tool supports over 100 languages.

item.title: DeepSeek previews new AI model that ‘closes the gap’ with frontier models
item.description: DeepSeek says both models are more efficient and performant than DeepSeek V3.2 due to architectural improvements, and have almost "closed the gap" with current leading models, both open and...

item.title: A new Republican privacy bill could be ‘worse than no standard at all’
item.description: Congress is once again attempting to pass a national data privacy law. But while it would introduce new protections in some states, it would weaken privacy rights in others - and it's missing...

item.title: The Download: supercharged scams and studying AI healthcare
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. We’re in a new era of AI-driven scams When ChatGPT was...

item.title: In another wild turn for AI chips, Meta signs deal for millions of Amazon AI CPUs
item.description: Meta has commandeered a big chunk of Amazon's homegrown CPUs (not GPUs) for AI agentic workloads, signaling that a new kind of chip race has begun.

item.title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court showdown will dish the dirt
item.description: Elon Musk cofounded OpenAI, and then flounced off in a huff when he wasn't anointed CEO, leaving Sam Altman as the last power-hungry man standing. Now, Musk is back with a lawsuit, and a trial is...

item.title: Bridging the AI Agent Authority Gap: Continuous Observability as the Decision Engine
item.description: The AI Agent Authority Gap - From Ungoverned to Delegation As discussed in our previous article, AI agents are exposing a structural gap in enterprise security, but the problem is often framed too...

item.title: 26 FakeWallet Apps Found on Apple App Store Targeting Crypto Seed Phrases
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a set of malicious apps on the Apple App Store that impersonate popular cryptocurrency wallets in an attempt to steal recovery phrases and private keys...

item.title: Instagram has launched another Snapchat clone
item.description: Instagram is testing a new dedicated app that's focused around Snapchat-like photo sharing features. The app, called "Instants," was launched in Italy and Spain yesterday, Business Insider...

item.title: Prestigious photo contest answers ‘what is a photo?’
item.description: We love to muse over how "real" photography is defined here at The Verge now that generative AI is so prolific, and the World Press Photo competition might have the answer. The prestigious award...

item.title: These AI Thirst Trap Creators Say They're Misunderstood
item.description: A viral red carpet moment shone light on a group of hunky Instagram influencers—and the followers who are too horny to care that they’re not real.

item.title: Tropic Trooper Uses Trojanized SumatraPDF and GitHub to Deploy AdaptixC2
item.description: Chinese-speaking individuals are the target of a new campaign that uses a trojanized version of SumatraPDF reader to deploy the AdaptixC2 Beacon post-exploitation agent and ultimately facilitate...

item.title: Health-care AI is here. We don’t know if it actually helps patients.
item.description: I don’t need to tell you that AI is everywhere. Or that it is being used, increasingly, in hospitals. Doctors are using AI to help them with notetaking. AI-based tools are trawling through patient...

item.title: 5 Reasons to Think Twice Before Using ChatGPT—or Any Chatbot—for Financial Advice
item.description: As people increasingly rely on AI chatbots for guidance, even on financial matters, a healthy dose of skepticism is critical.

item.title: LMDeploy CVE-2026-33626 Flaw Exploited Within 13 Hours of Disclosure
item.description: A high-severity security flaw in LMDeploy, an open-source toolkit for compressing, deploying, and serving LLMs, has come under active exploitation in the wild less than 13 hours after its public...
